calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Modifying Administrative Provisions Of Trust In Changed Circumstances
Off Calendar Oct-24-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gary Botto for (trustees) Paul Wattis Jr. & Carol Casey.

The Court stated that this is a petition to modify the trusts, that the trusts speak to succession and that it is not clear why the provisions should be disregarded. Mr. Botto stated that the last co-trustee, Wells Fargo Bank, has resigned, that Paul and Carol are now the sole trustees, that they do not have power to name successor trustees and that they both have experience and good sense to put into effect. The Court stated that the trustor wanted an institutional trustee. Mr. Botto stated that the provisions were drafted in 1956, that at that time it was believed in designating corporate trustees, that they used Wells Fargo Bank as a last resort, that Wells Fargo became the sole trustee and then took on Paul and Carol as co-trustees, that Wells Fargo no recognizes that the family assets and asset allocations did not fit into their plan, that Paul and Carol are engaged as trustees and that they are also trustees of trusts much larger than these. The Court stated that it is not questioning their ability to manage the trusts. Mr. Botto stated that the trusts should stay in the families but that they have no ability to appoint a successor trustee. The Court stated that it is here to protect the trust. Mr. Botto stated that the Court is here to protect the administrator of the trust, that if Paul cannot appoint a successor they would have to come back to court, that it makes no sense, cited 17209 and that it makes sense to get this in place. The Court took the petitions for order modifying administrative provisions of trust in changed circumstances under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

Petition To Accept Resignation Of Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. As Co-Trustee Of Trust For The Benefit Of Carol W. Casey; For Approval Of Account And Report; For Confirmation Of Acts, Transactions, And Proceedings; And For Instructions As To Delivery Of Trust Assets Upon Resignation
Off Calendar Jul-08-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dominic Campisi for petitioner Wells Fargo Bank; Setphanie Dobozin for Paul Wattis, Jr. and Carol Casey.

Ms. Dobozin stated that the proposed co-trustees are both professional fiduciaries, and that under the terms of the trust agreement their only ability to invade the trust principal is for the health, support and maintenance of the beneficiary. She argued that because of this limitation on expenditure of the funds of the trust, there would be no conflict of interest. Mr. Campisi stated that if there was a need to invade the trust corpus, there was a standard in place to examine the propriety of the expenditure. He added that if the Court was concerned regarding the co-trustees invasion of the trust, he could require that any such invasion require prior court approval. He stated that the bank is willing to remain on in a custodial capacity. He also added that the bank is paying Mr. Campisi's fees for the petition to accept its resignation. The Court stated that some of the argument presented at the hearing was not in the papers before the Court, and requested a supplemental submission of the arguments of counsel to be received by June 27, 2016. The Court will deem the matter under submission as of June 27, 2016. Parties to submit proposed orders.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kevin Macksoud. (Not reported)
